originalTitle: Fire kills five at Antwerp apartment block
summary: A man was seen clambering through a window to escape black smoke in the block where some 200 people live.
content:
Video of the incident showed a person on a balcony being helped into a neighbouring window away from the smoke

Five people have died and several others have been injured in a fire that broke out in a 10-storey block of flats in Antwerp, Belgian police say.

Thick smoke was seen billowing from the eighth floor of the building in the Linkeroever district, after the fire broke out just before 10:00 (08:00 GMT) on Wednesday.

Dramatic video showed a man clambering from his balcony through a neighbour's window to escape the black smoke.

Police say more than 200 people live in the block and the fire department evacuated 80 flats in the building.

They say the fire was caused by a technical problem on the ground floor.

"It has claimed a heavy toll here at Linkeroever," police spokeswoman Kim Bastiaens told Belgian public TV, adding that a number of people had suffered from serious and minor injuries.

Police had earlier spoken of six deaths but later revised the number down to five and during the afternoon the Antwerp fire service said the fire had been put out.

Philippe, King of the Belgians, later travelled to Linkeroever to meet some of the residents evacuated to a residential care centre and visit the scene of the fire.

Resident Geert Dewulf told VRT how thick smoke had stopped him getting out of his flat: "We barricaded ourselves into our flat and waited on the balcony. The fire brigade came to rescue us from the balcony 10 minutes or so later with their ladder."

Smoke was seen billowing from the top three floors of the 10-storey block

Firefighters said they were going from flat to flat to search for possible victims, explaining that the smoke had spread quickly through the building, making it harder to evacuate people.

Another man said his parents-in-law, one of them ex-mayor of Antwerp Bob Cools, had been successfully evacuated from the building and taken to hospital.

"We tried our utmost to save the cat but unfortunately in the end we were unable to," he said.

Antwerp fire brigade spokeswoman Marie de Clercq said it was a complex fire and the poor visibility and thick smoke in the building had made it difficult to put out.

Prime Minister Bart De Wever said his thoughts were with the victims and the evacuated residents of the "terrible fire" in Linkeroever.

The Flemish parliament began its session on Wednesday afternoon with a minute's silence for the victims of the fire.

Firefighters went from flat to flat in the apartment building, in the Linkeroever area of Antwerp
chineseTitle: 安特卫普公寓楼火灾造成五人死亡
chineseSummary: 在大约 200 人居住的街区，有人看到一名男子爬窗躲避黑烟。
chineseContent:
事件的视频显示，阳台上的一个人被帮助进入邻近的窗户，远离烟雾

比利时警方称，安特卫普一栋 10 层公寓楼发生火灾，造成 5 人死亡、数人受伤。

周三 10:00（格林威治标准时间 08:00）前发生火灾后，Linkeroever 区大楼的八楼冒出滚滚浓烟。

戏剧性的视频显示，一名男子从邻居的窗户爬出阳台，以躲避黑烟。

警方称，该街区居住着 200 多人，消防部门疏散了大楼内 80 套公寓。

他们说火灾是由底层的技术问题引起的。

警方发言人金·巴斯蒂安斯 (Kim Bastiaens) 告诉比利时公共电视台，“林克罗埃弗 (Linkeroever) 造成严重伤亡”，并补充说，许多人受了重伤和轻伤。

警方早些时候称有六人死亡，但后来将数字减少到五人，下午安特卫普消防局表示火灾已被扑灭。

比利时国王菲利普随后前往林克罗埃弗会见了一些疏散到住宅护理中心的居民，并参观了火灾现场。

居民Geert Dewulf告诉VRT，浓烟阻止了他走出公寓：“我们把自己关在公寓里，在阳台上等待。大约10分钟后，消防队用梯子从阳台上救了我们。”

这座10层大楼的顶三层冒出滚滚浓烟

消防队员表示，他们正在从一个公寓到另一个公寓寻找可能的受害者，并解释说烟雾已迅速在建筑物中蔓延，使得疏散人员变得更加困难。

另一名男子表示，他的岳父母，其中一位是安特卫普前市长鲍勃·库尔斯，已成功从大楼撤离并被送往医院。

“我们尽了最大努力去救这只猫，但不幸的是最终我们没能成功，”他说。

安特卫普消防队发言人玛丽·德·克莱克表示，这是一场复杂的火灾，能见度差，建筑物内浓烟滚滚，难以扑灭。

总理巴特·德韦弗表示，他对林克罗埃弗“可怕火灾”的受害者和疏散居民表示同情。

弗拉芒议会周三下午开始开会，为火灾遇难者默哀一分钟。

消防队员在安特卫普林克罗埃弗地区的公寓楼里从一个公寓到另一个公寓
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
